ai_insights.executive_summary: The TNO network delivered a composite performance score of 60.4 out of 100 (where 50 represents the network average) in February 2026, reflecting solid operational discipline across most clinics. Scheduler Compliance reached 80.0%, marginally above the company average of 79.97%, while average patient delays of 8.9 minutes sit well below the company average of 8.92 minutes, indicating effective patient flow management. However, two clinics—Cookeville and EPCBC—warrant focused attention due to outsized delays and scheduling gaps.
highlights: Smyrna continues to lead the network with a performance score of 85 out of 100, combining scheduler compliance of 89.7% (9.7 percentage points above the company average of 80.0%) with an average delay of just 7.0 minutes and chair utilization of 79.8%, demonstrating that high compliance and operational efficiency reinforce each other. Cleveland achieved similarly strong results with a score of 83 out of 100, posting 84.0% scheduler compliance alongside a 3.4-minute average delay—among the fastest in the network—and chair utilization of 81.2%, well positioned against the Onco benchmark of 67.8%. Tullahoma and Columbia represent emerging strengths: Tullahoma's chair utilization reached 90.8% (33.6 percentage points above the company average of 57.2%) with minimal overtime, while Columbia's 89.2% chair utilization demonstrates effective asset deployment even as the network normalizes overbooking toward the 100% threshold.
concerns: Cookeville presents the most pressing operational challenge, with an average delay of 22.6 minutes—a 153% gap above the company average of 8.9 minutes—despite respectable scheduler compliance of 88.0%. This disconnect between compliance and delay suggests the scheduling templates may not align with actual treatment duration or patient arrival patterns; we recommend reviewing Cookeville's scheduling intervals and iOptimize recommendations to identify whether templates underestimate procedure times or overlap bookings. EPCBC requires immediate intervention across multiple dimensions: scheduler compliance of 65.9% (14.1 percentage points below the company average of 80.0%), average delay of 19.6 minutes (120% above company average), and critically, iAssign utilization of only 56.0% against near-universal 100% adoption elsewhere in the network. We recommend an audit of EPCBC's iAssign deployment—whether staff training, system access, or process barriers are limiting adoption—as full adoption often correlates with improved scheduling discipline and patient flow.
recommendations: We recommend that you partner with Cookeville's leadership to conduct a targeted scheduling template audit, specifically examining whether current iOptimize intervals account for actual chair turnover time and patient check-in variability; this surgical intervention is more likely to yield rapid improvement than broad workflow changes. Simultaneously, engage EPCBC to diagnose barriers to iAssign adoption (staff confidence, system usability, or competing workflows), with the goal of piloting full adoption over the next 30 days—resolving this gap could unlock the 14+ percentage-point compliance lift the data suggests is achievable.

scheduler_compliance.label: Scheduler Compliance
scheduler_compliance.explanation: Percentage of appointments scheduled following iOptimize recommendations. Higher is better. Company average is typically 60-70%. OncoSmart benchmark target is 75%.

avg_delay_mins.label: Avg Delay
avg_delay_mins.explanation: Average daily schedule delay in minutes. Lower is better. Measures how far behind the clinic is running on average.

avg_treatments_per_day.label: Tx Past Close/Day
avg_treatments_per_day.explanation: Average treatments running past treatment close per day (overtime patients per day). Lower is better.

avg_treatment_mins_per_patient.label: Tx Mins Past Close/Patient
avg_treatment_mins_per_patient.explanation: Average treatment minutes past closing time per patient. Lower is better.

avg_chair_utilization.label: Chair Utilization
avg_chair_utilization.explanation: Average chair utilization rate. Higher is better.

iassign_utilization.label: iAssign Utilization
iassign_utilization.explanation: Percentage of nurse assignments completed via iAssign. Higher means more consistent, optimized assignments.

avg_patients_per_nurse.label: Patients/Nurse/Day
avg_patients_per_nurse.explanation: Average patients per nurse per day. Context-dependent -- too high means understaffing, too low means inefficiency.

avg_chairs_per_nurse.label: Chairs/Nurse
avg_chairs_per_nurse.explanation: Average chairs assigned per nurse. Context-dependent.

avg_nurse_to_patient_chair_time.label: Nurse Utilization
avg_nurse_to_patient_chair_time.explanation: Average nurse-to-patient in-chair time per day. Higher is better.

chatbot_context.data_notes: Company Avg rows represent the network average across all locations for this client in the given month. Onco rows are global oncology benchmarks from the OncoSmart network. Rows named Global Avg, Network Avg, Total, or Overall are network-wide aggregates, not individual clinic locations. MoM (Month-over-Month) deltas compare the current month to the prior month. Composite scores are weighted 0 to 100 where 100 is best-in-class performance.
